title: Email invite integration via Resend
description: Create a Next.js API route at POST /api/send-invite that sends a share request magic-link email to the recipient using the Resend SDK. The email should include the requester company's name, what's being requested (field count, document count), and the share link. TASK-2 already calls this endpoint; this task makes it functional.
acceptanceCriteria: resend package is installed in package.json
RESEND_API_KEY environment variable is read in the route
POST /api/send-invite accepts { recipientEmail, requesterName, shareLink, fieldCount, documentCount } in JSON body
Route sends an email from a configured from address to recipientEmail
Email subject: '{requesterName} is requesting your business information'
Email body (HTML) includes: requester name, brief description of what's requested, a prominent CTA button linking to shareLink
Route returns 200 on success, 400 if required fields missing, 500 on Resend error
If RESEND_API_KEY is not set, route returns 503 with a clear error message
tsc reports no new type errors

steps.details: Run: npm install resend. Verify it appears in package.json dependencies.
Create src/app/api/send-invite/route.ts. Export async function POST(request: Request). Parse the JSON body. Validate required fields: recipientEmail, requesterName, shareLink.
At the top of the handler: const apiKey = process.env.RESEND_API_KEY; if (!apiKey) return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Email service not configured' }, { status: 503 }).

steps.description: Send the email using Resend SDK
steps.details: import { Resend } from 'resend'. const resend = new Resend(apiKey). Call resend.emails.send({ from: 'Ramply <noreply@ramply.com>', to: [recipientEmail], subject: `${requesterName} is requesting your business information`, html: buildEmailHtml({ requesterName, shareLink, fieldCount, documentCount }) }). Define buildEmailHtml as a local function returning an HTML string with: a heading, paragraph explaining the request, and an <a href={shareLink}> button styled with inline CSS.
Add RESEND_API_KEY=your_key_here to the .env.local.example file (or create one if it doesn't exist). Do NOT commit actual API keys.
Run npx tsc --noEmit and npx eslint --fix src/app/api/send-invite/route.ts. Fix all errors.

technicalNotes: The from address 'noreply@ramply.com' requires a verified domain in Resend. For development/testing, use 'onboarding@resend.dev' which is Resend's sandbox address
Do not use a React email template library — plain HTML string is sufficient and keeps dependencies minimal
The route is a Next.js App Router route handler (not Pages Router API route)
